evals.prompt: Check for conflicting ESLint rules in this project
evals.expected_output: Should find no-hooks vs require-hook (Type A), non-nullable-type-assertion-style conflicts (Type B + C), padding-around-all supersession (Type C). Should NOT report prefer-to-be-truthy or prefer-to-be-falsy as conflicts since those rules are not active.

assertions.name: finds-no-hooks-vs-require-hook
assertions.text: Reports vitest/no-hooks vs vitest/require-hook as a conflict

assertions.name: no-false-positive-prefer-to-be-truthy
assertions.text: Does NOT report prefer-to-be-truthy as an active conflict (it is not enabled)

assertions.name: no-false-positive-prefer-to-be-falsy
assertions.text: Does NOT report prefer-to-be-falsy as an active conflict (it is not enabled)

assertions.name: no-hybrid-types
assertions.text: Does NOT use hybrid type labels like 'A+B' — uses exactly one of A, B, C, D per finding

assertions.name: has-summary-table
assertions.text: Ends with a summary table showing counts per conflict type

evals.prompt: Are there any vitest ESLint rules that conflict with each other in this project?
evals.expected_output: Should identify no-hooks vs require-hook. Should identify padding-around-all superseding individual padding rules. Should NOT report rules that are not in the active rule set (e.g. prefer-to-be-truthy, prefer-called-once, prefer-import-in-mock).

assertions.name: lists-active-vitest-rules
assertions.text: Shows or references the resolved list of active vitest rules before checking conflicts

assertions.name: finds-no-hooks-require-hook
assertions.text: Identifies no-hooks vs require-hook as mutually exclusive

assertions.name: no-inactive-rule-conflicts
assertions.text: Does NOT report conflicts involving prefer-to-be-truthy, prefer-to-be-falsy, prefer-called-once, prefer-called-with, or prefer-import-in-mock

evals.prompt: Audit the ESLint config for circular auto-fix issues
evals.expected_output: Should find non-nullable-type-assertion-style producing x! which violates no-non-null-assertion. Should NOT report circular fixes between rules that aren't both active.

assertions.name: finds-type-assertion-circular-fix
assertions.text: Identifies non-nullable-type-assertion-style auto-fix producing x! that violates no-non-null-assertion

assertions.name: only-active-rules
assertions.text: Does NOT report circular fixes involving inactive rules

assertions.name: uses-type-b-label
assertions.text: Labels the circular fix finding as Type B (not A+B or other hybrid)
